title: Get a termination of services or layoff certificate
description: Employers complete this form to give to a worker who has stopped working.
summary: When an employee stops working, their employer must complete a Termination of Services or Layoff Certificate. This is required under Regulation 47 of the National Insurance and Social Security Act.

The certificate is the employer’s formal statement that the employee has stopped working, why they have stopped, and how much they were earning in the last 12 weeks. The employee then uses it to claim unemployment benefit.
Employers should complete it promptly. The longer it takes, the longer the former employee has to wait for benefit payments to start.
The completed certificate can either be returned to the National Insurance Office directly or given to the worker to take with them when they apply.
who_can_apply: the employer of someone who has stopped working
a worker can ask their former employer for the certificate

items: the worker’s name, address, National Insurance Number and National Registration Number
the reason the worker stopped working
the worker’s earnings for the last 12 weeks of work

documents.intro: The employer will need to provide:

items.name: Payroll records for the last 12 weeks

how_long: Most employers can complete the certificate within a week.

options.heading: By post or in person
content: The completed certificate must be returned to the National Insurance Office, or given to the worker to take with them.

assumptions: ‘Most employers can complete the certificate within a week’ is an estimate.
‘There is no fee’ is inferred from the form not stating one.
